county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1850; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Raleigh County Clerk (Beckley). Formed 1850 from Fayette. Major coal county -- Beckley Exhibition Coal Mine documents mining heritage. Coal company records essential. New River Gorge area. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Raleigh County Historical Society and Beckley Area Foundation. Check Fayette for pre-1850 records. African American miners well documented. Tamarack cultural center has regional heritage resources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1787; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Randolph County Clerk (Elkins). Major county formed 1787 from Harrison. Elkins was a major railroad and timber center. Davis & Elkins College archives. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Randolph County Historical Society. Good surviving records. Major timber industry -- Western Maryland Railway records. Check Harrison for pre-1787 records. Civil War activity at Rich Mountain (1861). Monongahela National Forest area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1843; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Ritchie County Clerk (Harrisville). Formed 1843 from Harrison, Lewis, and Wood. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s -- among WV's earliest petroleum-producing counties. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Ritchie County Historical Society. Check parent counties for earlier records. North Bend State Park area. Good surviving records. Small population makes family tracing manageable.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1856; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Roane County Clerk (Spencer). Formed 1856 from Kanawha, Jackson, and Gilmer. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Oil and gas industry. Roane County Historical Society. Check parent counties for pre-1856 records. Spencer area families. Good surviving records. Rural county with timber and agricultural heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county formed 1871; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Summers County Clerk (Hinton). Formed 1871 (post-statehood) from Greenbrier, Monroe, Mercer, and Fayette. Hinton was a major C&O Railroad town -- railroad records important. Bluestone Lake area -- Army Corps of Engineers relocation records. Summers County Historical Society. Pre-formation records in parent counties. New River Gorge area. Good surviving records from formation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1844; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Taylor County Clerk (Grafton). Formed 1844 from Harrison, Barbour, and Marion. Grafton was a major B&O Railroad junction and site of the first Mother's Day observance (1908). First Union soldier killed in the Civil War (T. Bailey Brown) at Grafton. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Taylor County Historical and Genealogical Society. Check parent counties for earlier records. Grafton National Cemetery records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.record_losses: 1872 courthouse fire destroyed most early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1856; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Tucker County Clerk (Parsons). BURNED COUNTY -- 1872 courthouse fire destroyed most early records. Formed 1856 from Randolph. Major timber county -- Parsons Pulp and Lumber Company and other logging companies well documented. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Check Randolph for pre-1856 records. Tucker County Historical Society. Canaan Valley and Blackwater Falls area. Substitute sources include state copies of land grants, census records, and tax lists at WV Archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1814; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Tyler County Clerk (Middlebourne). Formed 1814 from Ohio County. Ohio River county with good surviving records. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Tyler County Historical Society. Check Ohio County for pre-1814 records. Sistersville oil boom (1890s) brought rapid population growth and immigrant workers. FamilySearch has microfilmed records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1851; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Upshur County Clerk (Buckhannon). Formed 1851 from Randolph, Barbour, and Lewis. WV Wesleyan College in Buckhannon has some local materials. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Upshur County Historical Society. Check parent counties for earlier records. Good surviving records. Timber and oil/gas industries. Stonewall Jackson Lake area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1842; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Wayne County Clerk (Wayne). Formed 1842 from Cabell. Ohio River county. Ceredo was a pre-Civil War planned community (abolitionist Eli Thayer). Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Wayne County Historical Society. Check Cabell for pre-1842 records. Good surviving records. Beech Fork State Park area. Kenova (at KENtucky-Ohio-Virginia border) area records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA
